description: A dark navy-bordered Department of Labor share-card in portrait format. The top two-thirds is a photograph of President Trump standing at a lectern in the U.S. Capitol (House chamber) wearing a navy suit and maroon/purple tie with an American flag lapel pin; behind him Vice President JD Vance (left) is clapping and Speaker Mike Johnson (center) stands, with American flags and ornate columns visible. Centered in large white serif all-caps is the headline 'PROTECTING AMERICANS'. Below, on a white rounded panel, is the masthead of 'The Charlotte Observer' (in its classic blue blackletter logo) lending the appearance of a news report, followed by serif body text: 'The Trump Administration is taking decisive action to ensure American job opportunities are not being replaced by cheap foreign labor.' and 'The Labor Department launched nearly 200 investigations into companies suspected of abusing the H-1B visa program as part of Project Firewall, an effort to ensure employers do not unlawfully displace American workers.' The DOL seal appears at the very bottom. The technique borrows a newspaper masthead to add third-party credibility to the administration's H-1B enforcement messaging, framing immigration/visa enforcement as protecting American jobs. Target audience is the public and DOL followers supportive of restricting foreign labor.
notable_text: PROTECTING AMERICANS; The Charlotte Observer; nearly 200 investigations ... abusing the H-1B visa program as part of Project Firewall
